title: What I did in my three month absense from youtube - Request

transcript.text: Here we've got a request video. Somebody, what was his name? I can't remember.
Wanted me to do a video about what I did for the last three months when I wasn't making videos and like why I stopped making videos and then why I started making videos again.
The reason I stopped is because I got so like immersed into working on my website.
And because whenever I switched up scenarios for myself, I have, I get sucked into my website and I have trouble finding a balance.
And so when I'm making videos, that means that I have a balance.
That means like when I'm making videos, that means I'm also exercising, you know.
But when I'm not making videos, you should worry about me because it means I'm in the computer the whole time.
But so what did I start? And also making videos at my mom's house, I don't like doing that because I talk to my mom a lot and I tell her what's going on.
Like I kind of have verbal diarrhea, you know, and so I'm like, yeah, mom, I did a vlog today and I talked about this.
And then, but, and so she's a normal person, you know, she's not crazy like me.
So she's like, oh no, why would you say that? Oh no, don't say that.
So then I'm being censored all the time and it just, it's just annoying because then she tries to get me to delete the video and it's just not worth it.
It's better to not make videos at all. Um, so then when I started moving here, I think I lived here for like two weeks before I started making videos again.
And, um, yeah, I mean, when I live, when I'm isolated, see my mom, I don't make videos with my mom because my mom is one of those mothers and people are probably going to be surprised by this.
No, I love her with all my heart. She's the best mother in the world.
But my mom is one of those mothers who is always, uh, trying to suppress you, you know, like not, not encouraging me, kind of like trying to make me paranoid.
And, um, so she actually does a really good job of making me paranoid and kind of trying to suppress me and shut me down and pretty much everything I do.
She didn't like my kayak trip. She didn't like how I was living in my van.
She doesn't like my timeline or anything about my website.
But doesn't like my YouTube videos. Basically doesn't like anything about me except for, you know, the fact that I'm her son and we hang out and stuff and we have fun together.
But she doesn't like anything about my lifestyle.
So, uh, yeah, it's just annoying when I, you know, I'm like, yeah, I did this.
And she's like, no, don't, no, you're crazy and stuff.
So, um, so, um, so I started doing them again because I, uh, see when I'm living by myself,
I'm not under like the, um, you know, the shade of my mom and I don't, and I don't, I don't get like paranoid at all when I'm by myself.
You know, I can get like weird, rude comments and shit and like lose all my friends or whatever.
And it doesn't matter because, you know, I have this bigger vision, you know, and I just focus on that.
So what was I doing?
Um, yeah, so I went home and I'm like from the beginning of January, I pretty much looked at my website and I said,
Okay, I'm going to like not do anything about that.
I'm not going to do any more.
Well, actually for like two days, I was finishing up what I was doing with the timeline with World War II, all the battles in World War II.
And then I looked at my website and I was like, okay, I'm going to abandon that timeline for now.
And I'm going to tie up all the loose ends of my personal website because there were all these pages that weren't finished and then the links didn't work and all that kind of stuff.
So I was basically just finishing up pages that already existed.
And then I kind of noticed that and that was like a month or something, you know, and I was there like three months.

So then I, uh, um, I realized that I wasn't really retaining a lot of what I was listening to in the audio books, you know, and, uh, I figured out the best way to retain stuff in audio books is just to listen to a wide range of books, listen to one book.
And then when you get excited, listen to something else and then you can listen to it and you don't have to actually active, listen to it, actively listen to it.

And I'm going to do a video on how I listen to audio books when I'm like, once I start doing it, when I'm exercising, you don't have to actually try to listen to it because you're automatically going to just absorb the shit.
That's interesting.
And so when you're listening to new audio books all the time, then you can always do that.

You know, if you try to listen to the same thing over and over again, the same audio book, like I was trying with the Gale of Wars, it's just, you're not going to retain anymore and it's just better to move on.

So I wanted to, I looked at all of my pages on my website, all of the different, uh, sections of, um, all the different chapters of all the different free books you can get from bookshouldbefree.com, read by people mostly.
And, uh, and then computers read some of them and then, uh, the Kindle, which reads to you, which are all free books.
And I think it's pretty, and it's interesting that Kindle had a totally different collection as the book should be free.
So, but I think I pretty much have, I have a good collection, so I pretty much have all the important books that anybody would want to, um,
Read if you're interested in history.
Um, so, uh, I decided that I had to make pages that had the titles of all the books as well as the contents of all the chapters of all the books.
And so I just put the nose to the grindstone and I worked and it took me all the way until, I don't know, a couple of weeks ago to do that.
And so that's all I was doing.

And I, like, literally, you know, I didn't go running, I didn't go outside, I didn't go downtown, I didn't go for a walk, I didn't, uh, go for a drive, I didn't hang out with anybody, I didn't watch any movies, didn't watch any TV.
I would, like, work all the time and then I, until I would, like, collapse.
And then as soon as I wake up, I didn't work again.
And I drove my mom batty because I was in the house all the time.
And then I was getting really grumpy and, like, snappy, like, with my brothers and stuff.
And, uh, yeah, that's what got me kicked out because I got drunk one night and, like, got angry and shit.
I, like, threw a plate and I was like, you leave me alone, fuck you.
And they're like, get the fuck out of here.
And I was like, all right.
So I left and, and that was actually a good idea.
Even though I have to live way the fuck out here in Lafayette, which sucks.
But I'm going to move back to Boulder in October.
So that's why I wasn't making videos, man.
I was just, I got sucked in.
And it happens a lot, like, it's, I'm, I'm, I have a disposition to do that.
I have a addictive personality, a neurotic personality.
So, like, when I was living in my van for a year and a half, all I was doing was working on my timeline, you know.
I wasn't exercising, I wasn't doing anything.
You know.
And it was weird.
Like, the day that my car broke down, it was the day that I was going to start exercising.
I was going to go to the gym that day.
I got up at six.
I was like, yeah, today's the, what the fuck, car.
Oh, shit.
I mean, if you don't believe me, go back and look at the, the V-log.
I did a morning V-log.
I'm like, yeah, I'm going to go to the gym today.
Because it was before the car blew up.
The car was, like, just about to blow up.
It was, like, January, like, 3rd of 2011 or something like that.
Yeah.
So, I mean, it took me forever.
So, anyways, that's one reason I want to live in Boulder.
Because I want to get into a rhythm where I can have a balance.
And, um, so that's why I started making the videos again.
And that's what I was up to.
I was just working.
